calendar.matters:
Petition For Withdrawal Of Funds From Blocked Account
Off Calendar Sep-19-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Martha Daetwyler for and with Tamara Rathod (Administrator).

Ms. Daetwyler stated that the primary estate is in Indiana, that the Decedent and the Administrator had a residence here, that the car (BMW) was an asset of the estate, that Ms. Rathod would not give the Attorney in Indiana any information they requested and that she would not turn over the vehicle. Ms. Daetwyler also stated that she was told Ms. Rathod was ultimately able to turn the car over into her name, that she sold the car, that it was six weeks when she finally got the petition for final distribution signed and back from the Client, and that there was a big push from the Indiana probate to get this probate closed. In addition, Ms. Daetwyler spoke of the complicated procedure with the bank, stated that it was anticipated when the Judge signed the order for final distribution that there would be a fair amount of additional work, that she did not feel the banks would have worked with the Paralegal because it was so complicated, and that since she started this petition to withdraw the funds from the blocked account, she has incurred fees in the amount of $9,000.00 that she will not be reimbursed for. Ms. Rathod stated that the car belonged to their company, that in order to sell the car the titles had to match, spoke of the difficult procedure with DMV to get the title on the car to match, stated that the car was not driven, that some of the Attorney's fees are inaccurate, and spoke of her trips to the bank and time she spent dealing with the bank. Ms. Rathod also stated that she would like to have the bank send the check directly to her due to issues she had with Ms. Daetwyler with regard to document exchange. Ms. Daetwyler stated that four checks will be issued by the bank and that she has no problem with Ms. Rathod's request. The Petition for Withdrawal of Funds from Blocked Account is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Ex Parte Petition To Advance Hearing Date
Off Calendar Jul-31-2018 Denied

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-24-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Martha Daetwyler for Tamara Wightman Rathod (Administrator). Appearing through is Court Call Janis Ann Carney (former attorney for the administrator). Appearing though Court Call Tamara Wightman Rathod (Administrator).

Ms. Daetwyler stated that the Court has requested invoices and receipts for reimbursement, that she did not receive them and they have not been submitted. Ms. Carney reported that she submitted her declaration, that the only matter is extraordinary fees, that there is a question of an $8.00 parking fee that she will defer to the Court and the other fees were reasonable and necessary.

Tamara stated that she has all of the receipts and is happy to provide them, but they are too large to email and she is unsure of what to do. The Court asked Ms. Daetwyler if she wanted the matter submitted or to wait a week for the documents to be presented to the Court and asked about the $102,000.00 reserve. Ms. Daetwyler responded that it is a smaller account, that there could be unknown expenses and possible taxes. Tamara argued that the account is well funded and that the final property was sold in Indiana.

Ms. Daetwyler reported that she spoke to counsel in Indiana and they are no in a position to release the funds. Tamara argued that there is an $80,000 tax return and other things funding the account and there is no reason for Indiana to hold funds. Ms. Daetwyler requested clarification on the procedure for blocked accounts and proposed that she receive the signed orders so she can deliver it to the bank providing FedEx envelopes. Tamara had objections to this proposal, that she would like to see final balances, that the accounts be closely monitored, that she wants another set of eyes on this, that she wants original statements not copies and that she can take the orders to the bank herself. Ms. Daetwyler stated that she intends to request final statements and that Tamara is no a resident of the Bay Area.

The Court will consider the proposal. The amended petition for final distribution is taken under submission.

Judge Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Nancy Wong/ Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. Not Reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1810 Green St., San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Jul-15-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Martha Daetwyler for and with (Administrator) Tamara Rathod. Yulissa Zalaica for (beneficiary) Masheka Rathod.

Ms. Zulaica stated that they are not here to contest, that her client has a couple of concerns and that they needed clarification on what was to happen with the proceeds of the sale. The court stated that there is an issue with the switch in realtors without court approval, that it has the request filed yesterday but there is nothing to suggest that notice was given. Ms. Daetwyler stated that notice was given to two beneficiaries and that she has consent of waiver of notice from the others. The Court stated that the more difficult issue is the protection of the proceeds and that it is expected that they will be placed in multiple blocked accounts. Ms. Daetwyler stated that there has not been time to look into this, that she would like for the funds to go into her firms trust account until they can be moved into blocked accounts and that she would make an effort to get the accounts set up prior to escrow. The court stated that there would be a loss of interest and that the money would not be secured and that the sale order will not be signed until the accounts are set up. Ms. Daetwyler suggested that the funds go to the administrator in Indiana. Ms. Zulaica stated that the funds could be held in escrow until the accounts are set up and that the property is headed for foreclosure. Ms. Daetwyler clarified how title would be held and that if notice had been done properly it would have gone to the 2 children and their attorney. Ms. Zulaica waived notice for Masheka and confirmed that the other beneficiary has been out of the country for some time and is not sure of the return date. The court stated that it would confirm the sale of the property conditioned on the proposed order designating blocked accounts at a sufficient number of banks, that the proposed order reflects a recognized form of title of buyer, that it recognizes waiver of notice by Masheka but needs to receive a waiver by the other beneficiary and that if they cannot get the waiver and want the court to waive notice they need to submit something to that effect. The sale was confirmed at $3,400,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to receipt/review of the order with the above referenced conditions).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
